disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Profound, disabling exhaustion began roughly three weeks ago, immediately after recovering from an uncomplicated cold/upper respiratory illness
trigger_question_examples: Was there anything going on around the time it began?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It came on about three weeks ago, right after I got over what seemed like a normal cold. I just never got my energy back.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Severe multi-day symptom crashes follow even minor physical or cognitive effort (post-exertional worsening) — a shower puts the patient in bed for two days
trigger_question_examples: What happens if you try to do something active?
How do you feel the day after you exert yourself?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: If I do anything at all — even a shower or concentrating hard — I completely crash and I'm stuck in bed for a day or two afterwards. It's like I get punished for any effort.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sleep is completely unrefreshing despite long hours asleep
trigger_question_examples: Do you wake up feeling rested?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I sleep a lot but I never wake up feeling any better — it does nothing for me.

trigger_question_examples: Have you noticed any problems with concentration or memory?
How is your thinking or focus?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My head is really foggy — I can't concentrate or think clearly like I used to.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Mild generalised muscle and joint aches without joint swelling or redness
trigger_question_examples: Any aches or pains in your muscles or joints?
Are any joints swollen, hot or red?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My muscles and joints ache a bit all over, but nothing is swollen or red — it's just a general achiness.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Intermittent subjective feeling of feverishness, but no temperature elevation when actually measured
trigger_question_examples: Do you feel feverish, and have you actually measured your temperature?
When you check your temperature, is it up?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I do feel hot and feverish on and off, but whenever I actually check the thermometer it's normal.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Palpitations occur only after consuming large amounts of caffeine; no palpitations, chest pain or breathlessness at rest
trigger_question_examples: Have you had any chest pain, breathlessness, or a racing or irregular heartbeat?
Do you get heart flutters, and when?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I drank tons of coffee trying to get some energy and it did nothing except give me heart flutters. At rest I don't get any chest pain, breathlessness or racing heart.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I don't think it's my heart — the flutters only happened when I overdid the caffeine.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Denies unintentional weight loss, drenching night sweats, and any limb weakness or focal neurological symptoms
trigger_question_examples: Have you lost weight without trying, or had drenching night sweats?
Any weakness in your arms or legs, or trouble speaking or seeing?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I haven't lost any weight, no drenching night sweats, and no weakness or numbness anywhere — nothing like that.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Lives alone with severe functional restriction and is highly distressed and anxious about the loss of function
trigger_question_examples: Who is around to help you at home?
How are you coping emotionally with all this?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I live on my own and I'm really struggling — I can barely look after myself and I'm frightened about what this means and whether I'll ever recover.
